questions.question: What is the conclusion of the argument: If it is raining, then the streets are wet. If the streets are wet, then the dog is shaking. What can be inferred?
options: C) The dog will not shake.
questions.explanation: This question requires application of Modus Ponens rule of inference.

questions.question: Given: All humans are mortal. Socrates is a human. What can be inferred about Socrates?
questions.explanation: This question requires application of Universal Instantiation rule of inference.

questions.question: What is the conclusion of the argument: If all students in a class are wearing blue shirts, then the teacher is wearing a blue shirt. The teacher is wearing a blue shirt. What can be inferred?
options: A) All students in a class are wearing blue shirts.
B) The teacher is wearing a blue shirt.
C) The teacher is not wearing a blue shirt.
questions.explanation: This question requires application of Modus Ponens rule of inference.

questions.question: Given: All cats are animals. All animals have four legs. What can be inferred about cats?
options: C) All cats have four legs.
questions.explanation: This question requires application of Universal Instantiation rule of inference.

questions.question: What is the conclusion of the argument: If it is sunny outside, then the temperature is high. If the temperature is high, then the pool will be open. What can be inferred about the pool?
options: A) The pool will be open.
B) The pool will be closed.
questions.explanation: This question requires application of Modus Ponens rule of inference.
